title: echinocereus cinerascens subsp. tulensis
intro: Echinocereus cinerascens subsp. tulensis, also known as the Tulane hedgehog cactus, is a rare species of cactus native to the southwestern United States. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds that can be extracted from its stems. The health benefits of Tulane hedgehog cactus include potential treatments for wounds, burns, and skin irritations due to its soothing and protective properties. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing its stems in water or oil, which can be used topically to harness its medicinal properties.
